assertionResults.reason: Default record import saved raw YAML and produced a separate auto-improved YAML artifact without overwriting the original.
evidence: diff evidence captured between raw and improved outputs (ordering mutation shown in diff -u output).

assertionResults.reason: Record flow with improve disabled preserved recording output and produced no improved YAML side-effect artifact.
When auto-improve write failed (intentional improved-path directory collision), the recording remained on disk and CLI surfaced manual next-step guidance.
evidence: auto-improve failure message observed: EISDIR illegal operation on improved output path
manual fallback guidance observed: 'You can run it manually: ui-test improve ... --plan && ... --apply-plan ...'

assertionResults.reason: Default record auto-apply output matched manual improve --plan + --apply-plan output for the same recorded YAML input.

frictions.description: Vitest include rules rejected /tmp test path, so validation test had to be placed under scripts/ to execute.
frictions.resolution: Ran the same flow-a test from scripts/flow-a-record-auto-improve.validation.test.mjs, then removed the temporary test file after execution.

commandsRun.observation: Targeted flow test passed; showed default auto-improve apply, no-improve bypass behavior, forced auto-improve failure fallback warning, and successful manual plan/apply convergence.
Confirmed non-overwrite behavior with visible raw-vs-improved diff output.
Files were identical (auto-apply output converged with plan/apply output).

commandsRun.command: file existence check for no-improve improved output and failure-case raw output
commandsRun.observation: No-improve improved artifact was absent; failure-case raw recording artifact was present.

summary: Validated 4 assigned assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
